informal: Suppose that $S, T \in \mathcal{L}(V)$ are such that $S T=T S$. Prove that $\operatorname{null} (T-\lambda I)$ is invariant under $S$ for every $\lambda \in \mathbf{F}$.
lean:
theorem Axler_exercise_5_4_corrected {F V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Field F]
    [Module F V] (S T : V →ₗ[F] V) (hST : S ∘ T = T ∘ S) (c : F) :
    Submodule.map S (ker (T - c • LinearMap.id)) ≤ ker (T - c • LinearMap.id)
reasoning: The informal statement assumes S,T are linear operators on V with ST=TS and concludes that null(T-λI) is invariant under S for every λ in F. The Lean version takes S T : V →ₗ[F] V over an arbitrary field F and module V, hypothesizes S ∘ T = T ∘ S (function composition equality, which for linear maps is equivalent to LinearMap composition equality), and for every c : F concludes Submodule.map S (ker (T - c • id)) ≤ ker (T - c • id), i.e. the kernel of T-cI is mapped into itself by S — exactly the invariance claim. The quantifier over c, the operator T - c • id corresponding to T - λI, and the use of ker as the null space all match. No extra or missing hypotheses are introduced.
